name: Hollgh
description: An angry or insulted ancestor spirit, invisible apart from liminal circumstances: shamans in trance, at the edge of sleep, in deep fog.
behavior: Attacks the senses - ringing ears, heavy limbs, dimming sight - and in numbers can cause totalsensory loss, leaving victims helpless to environmental dangers.
habitat: Barrow-mounds, old vaults, and any ground where the dead were buried carelessly or had their rest disturbed.

name: Ongkhan-hollgh
description: A bound spirit-helper gone feral and parasitic, contained within a vessel, polluting its surroundings.
behavior: Passively drains vitality from those nearby, causing rust, foul dreams, and sickness, while compelling people to touch or open its vessel, which leads to madness.
habitat: Abandoned shaman's huts, reliquaries in ruins, or unknowingly carried by traders as curious carvings or stoppered jars.
weakness: unknown: destroying the vessel does not kill the spirit

name: Ezn
description: An ancient and powerful master spirit of a place; it IS the place, its name cannot be known.
behavior: Does not act directly but when offended turns its domain hostile—streams go bitter, paths loop endlessly, weather turns cruel.
habitat: Bound to a singular geographic feature such as a mountain, megalith, deep bog, or cave; they never wander.
weakness: specific offerings or ritual silences, though the terms vary by Ezn and guessing wrong is itself a trespass
